meta.certificationName: AWS Certified Generative AI Developer – Professional
meta.title: AWS-GAP-1: Bedrock Application Delivery and Governance

questions.stem: A team wants a chat application that can maintain a single request/response interaction with an Amazon Bedrock model using the newer conversational API. Which API is the best fit?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because `Converse` is the Bedrock API designed for conversational interactions and supports messages plus inference configuration in a consistent request shape.

**B** is incorrect because streaming can be useful, but it is not the primary newer conversational API abstraction.

**C** is incorrect because async invocation is for long-running asynchronous inference jobs, not ordinary chat turns.

**D** is incorrect because model customization jobs are for tuning, not inference.

[Amazon Bedrock Converse API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/conversation-inference-call.html)

questions.stem: An application needs one-off text generation against a specific model without maintaining a chat message structure. Which Bedrock API should the developer choose?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because `InvokeModel` is the core inference API for sending a request directly to a foundation model.

**B** is incorrect because guardrails moderate content but do not replace model inference.

**C** is incorrect because retrieve-and-generate is for RAG workflows, not a basic direct prompt.

**D** is incorrect because prompt router is a separate routing capability, not the base inference call.

[Amazon Bedrock InvokeModel](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/inference-invoke.html)

questions.stem: A developer is selecting a foundation model for a new workload. Which factors are most important to evaluate first? (Select TWO)

options.text: Whether the model supports the required modalities
The default VPC CIDR block for the account
The Region where Amazon S3 was first used

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because model selection should begin with workload requirements such as modality support and acceptable latency/cost trade-offs.

**C** is incorrect because network addressing is unrelated to model capability selection.

**D** is incorrect because historical S3 usage does not determine model suitability.

[Amazon Bedrock model selection guidance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/models-features.html)

questions.stem: A team wants to let an Amazon Bedrock agent call a private business API. What is the supported Bedrock pattern for this integration?

options.text: Create an action group backed by AWS Lambda
Mount the API server directly inside the foundation model
Attach an Amazon S3 bucket as the only tool
Use a CloudFormation stack as a conversational tool at runtime

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Bedrock Agents use action groups, and Lambda is the standard way to implement tool execution logic for agent actions.

**B** is incorrect because models cannot directly host arbitrary private APIs.

**C** is incorrect because S3 stores content but is not a general action execution mechanism.

**D** is incorrect because CloudFormation is not a Bedrock agent runtime tool interface.

[Amazon Bedrock Agents action groups](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/agents-action-add.html)

questions.stem: A developer wants Bedrock to answer from approved enterprise documents stored in Amazon S3 with managed retrieval. Which feature should be implemented?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Knowledge Bases for Bedrock provide managed ingestion, embeddings, storage integration, and retrieval for RAG patterns.

**B** is incorrect because provisioned throughput addresses capacity, not document retrieval.

**C** is incorrect because inference profiles route model invocations, not document retrieval pipelines.

**D** is incorrect because workflows can orchestrate steps but do not provide managed RAG by themselves.

[Knowledge Bases for Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/knowledge-base.html)

questions.stem: A company must block topics such as medical advice and redact PII before model output is returned to end users. Which Bedrock feature should be configured?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Bedrock Guardrails support denied topics, content filters, and sensitive information filtering such as PII redaction.

**B** is incorrect because CloudWatch alarms observe metrics but do not moderate responses.

**C** is incorrect because Macie classifies S3 data, not live model prompts and responses.

**D** is incorrect because Config checks resource compliance, not model output safety.

[Amazon Bedrock Guardrails](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/guardrails.html)

questions.stem: A team is designing a RAG application and wants to reduce hallucinations. Which actions are most aligned with AWS guidance? (Select TWO)

options.text: Ground responses in retrieved source content
Evaluate outputs with domain-specific test sets
Increase temperature to maximize creativity for all prompts
Disable citations to avoid exposing sources

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because grounding with retrieved evidence and evaluating with representative datasets are standard controls for reducing unsupported answers.

**C** is incorrect because higher temperature usually increases variability, which can worsen factual consistency.

**D** is incorrect because citations help users inspect grounding rather than reducing hallucinations.

[AWS guidance for building reliable RAG applications](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/knowledge-base.html)

questions.stem: A workload sends the same long system prompt and policy context on nearly every request. Which Bedrock capability can reduce repeated token processing cost and latency?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because prompt caching is designed to reuse repeated prompt context so the application does not pay the full cost of reprocessing unchanged content every time.

**B** is incorrect because customization changes model behavior but does not solve repeated prompt overhead per request.

**C** is incorrect because denied topics are safety controls, not performance optimizations.

**D** is incorrect because batch inference is for asynchronous large-scale jobs, not repeated interactive prompts.

[Prompt caching in Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/prompt-caching.html)

questions.stem: A production chatbot has highly predictable traffic and strict latency SLOs. Which Bedrock option is most appropriate?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Provisioned Throughput reserves model inference capacity to provide more predictable throughput and latency characteristics for steady workloads.

**B** is incorrect because Lambda might host app code, but it does not reserve model capacity.

**C** is incorrect because Transfer Acceleration is unrelated to model inference.

**D** is incorrect because Glue streaming jobs are not a Bedrock capacity feature.

[Provisioned Throughput for Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/prov-throughput.html)

questions.stem: A team wants AWS-managed workflows for comparing model outputs with automatic metrics and optional human review. Which Bedrock capability should they use?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Bedrock model evaluation supports automated and human-based evaluation workflows for comparing model quality and safety.

**B** is incorrect because cross-Region inference affects routing, not evaluation.

**C** is incorrect because knowledge bases support retrieval, not model comparison workflows.

**D** is incorrect because prompt caching optimizes reuse, not quality measurement.

[Amazon Bedrock model evaluation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-evaluation.html)

questions.stem: A developer needs token-by-token responses in a chat UI. Which Bedrock approach best supports this?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because `ConverseStream` streams generated content incrementally for conversational applications.

**B** is incorrect because knowledge base creation is administrative setup, not inference streaming.

**C** is incorrect because availability lookup does not return generated tokens.

**D** is incorrect because listing guardrails is unrelated to response streaming.

[Amazon Bedrock ConverseStream](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/conversation-inference-call.html)

questions.stem: A team wants a single application layer that can swap among supported Bedrock models while keeping a common conversational request shape. Which API best enables that portability?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because `Converse` normalizes conversational requests across supported models, improving application portability.

**B** is incorrect because customization jobs do not define the runtime request interface.

**C** is incorrect because S3 Select queries objects, not models.

**D** is incorrect because AWS Batch is unrelated.

[Amazon Bedrock Converse API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/conversation-inference-call.html)

questions.stem: Which statements about Knowledge Bases for Amazon Bedrock are accurate? (Select TWO)

options.text: They can use Amazon S3 as a data source
They are intended for managed RAG retrieval workflows
They require the developer to manually implement every embedding call
They replace IAM authorization for Bedrock access

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Knowledge Bases support managed retrieval workflows and can ingest from sources such as Amazon S3.

**C** is incorrect because the service can manage embedding and retrieval plumbing for supported patterns.

**D** is incorrect because IAM is still required for access control.

[Knowledge Bases for Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/knowledge-base.html)

questions.stem: An organization wants an agent to call multiple business tools based on user intent. What should the developer configure?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Agents can be extended with multiple action groups so they can invoke different tools for different tasks.

**B** is incorrect because bucket versioning does not define agent tools.

**C** is incorrect because endpoint policies govern access, not intent routing.

**D** is incorrect because health checks are unrelated.

[Amazon Bedrock Agents action groups](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/agents-action-add.html)

questions.stem: Which AWS control is most directly designed to prevent unsafe model outputs from being returned to users in real time?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Guardrails are applied during inference to screen prompts and responses according to configured policies.

**B** is incorrect because lifecycle rules govern object retention.

**C** is incorrect because Auto Scaling manages compute fleets, not model output policy.

**D** is incorrect because Athena workgroups govern SQL usage.

[Amazon Bedrock Guardrails](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/guardrails.html)

questions.stem: A security team is reviewing a GenAI application on AWS. Which practices best align with responsible AI and data protection guidance? (Select TWO)

options.text: Use least-privilege IAM permissions for Bedrock integrations
Evaluate prompts and outputs for harmful content
Grant broad administrator permissions to simplify experimentation
Store sensitive prompts in public read-only S3 buckets

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because least-privilege access and safety evaluation are core controls for secure and responsible GenAI deployments.

**C** is incorrect because broad permissions increase risk.

**D** is incorrect because public storage for sensitive prompts is unsafe.

[Security in Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/security.html)

questions.stem: Which metric destination would a team typically use to monitor Bedrock-powered application behavior over time on AWS?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because CloudWatch is the standard AWS service for application metrics, logs, and alarms.

**B** is incorrect because Resolver handles DNS resolution.

**C** is incorrect because Artifact distributes compliance reports.

**D** is incorrect because Organizations manages accounts.

[Monitoring AWS resources with Amazon CloudWatch](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/WhatIsCloudWatch.html)

questions.stem: A prompt change appears to lower answer quality for common support questions. What is the best way to detect that regression before broad release?

options.text: Run evaluation against a representative benchmark set
Rotate the KMS key for the application

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because regression testing with representative prompts and expected outcomes is the direct way to measure whether quality declined.

**B** is incorrect because temperature changes behavior but does not measure regression.

**C** is incorrect because disabling logs removes useful evidence.

**D** is incorrect because KMS rotation is good hygiene but not a quality test.

[Amazon Bedrock model evaluation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-evaluation.html)

questions.stem: A team is evaluating a RAG assistant. Which metrics are most useful for this evaluation? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because RAG evaluation focuses on whether retrieved context is relevant and whether answers remain faithful to that context.

**C** is incorrect because subnet utilization is infrastructure telemetry, not answer quality.

**D** is incorrect because multipart upload count is unrelated to retrieval quality.

[Amazon Bedrock model evaluation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-evaluation.html)

questions.stem: Which Bedrock feature is specifically intended to help build retrieval-augmented generation applications with managed data connectors and retrieval?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Knowledge Bases are the managed Bedrock feature for RAG-style retrieval from approved content.

**B** is incorrect because guardrails moderate content.

**C** is incorrect because provisioned throughput reserves capacity.

**D** is incorrect because StackSets deploy infrastructure, not RAG.

[Knowledge Bases for Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/knowledge-base.html)

questions.stem: An agent must invoke an order-status API that requires custom business logic, authentication checks, and response normalization. Which implementation is most appropriate?

options.text: Back the action group with AWS Lambda
Put the API URL inside the user prompt
Train a custom foundation model to memorize order data

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Lambda provides the controlled execution layer for Bedrock agent tools, including authentication, transformation, and downstream API calls.

**B** is incorrect because prompt text alone does not create a secure callable tool.

**C** is incorrect because memorizing operational data in a model is unsafe and inflexible.

**D** is incorrect because event notifications do not execute agent tools.

[Amazon Bedrock Agents action groups](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/agents-action-add.html)

questions.stem: A company wants to screen user prompts for disallowed topics before any model response is generated. Which feature should be enabled?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Guardrails can inspect prompts and enforce denied topic policies before or during inference handling.

**B** is incorrect because Object Lock is for WORM storage.

**C** is incorrect because Vault Lock protects backup retention policies.

**D** is incorrect because snapshot restore is unrelated.

[Amazon Bedrock Guardrails](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/guardrails.html)

questions.stem: Which actions can reduce inference cost for a production GenAI application on AWS? (Select TWO)

options.text: Reuse repeated context with prompt caching
Increase output token limits for all requests
Duplicate every request across two models by default

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because right-sizing the model and reusing repeated context are direct cost controls.

**C** is incorrect because higher output limits typically increase cost.

**D** is incorrect because duplicating requests doubles spend unless there is a specific testing need.

[Prompt caching in Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/prompt-caching.html)

questions.stem: A chatbot gives different answers for the same question after a model upgrade. What should the team do first?

options.text: Run regression evaluation on the old and new model outputs
Move the application to another Region

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because comparing old and new outputs on a fixed benchmark is the fastest way to identify whether the upgrade changed quality or safety.

**B** is incorrect because Lambda memory may affect app runtime, not model quality drift.

**C** is incorrect because deleting the knowledge base removes evidence and functionality.

**D** is incorrect because Region changes do not explain the regression.

[Amazon Bedrock model evaluation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-evaluation.html)

questions.stem: Which statements are true about foundation model selection in Amazon Bedrock? (Select TWO)

options.text: Different models support different modalities and use cases
Latency and pricing can vary by model
All Bedrock models behave identically for every task
Every model is available in every Region by default

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because model capabilities and commercial characteristics differ, so selection must account for workload needs.

**C** is incorrect because model behavior differs across providers and architectures.

**D** is incorrect because availability varies by model and Region.

[Amazon Bedrock base model features](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/models-features.html)

questions.stem: A team wants to process a large backlog of documents with Bedrock without blocking interactive users. Which pattern is most appropriate?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because batch inference is the fit for large asynchronous processing workloads rather than interactive request/response usage.

**B** is incorrect because browser loops are inefficient and interactive.

**C** is incorrect because word filters do not process documents.

**D** is incorrect because Parameter Store stores configuration only.

[Batch inference in Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/batch-inference.html)

questions.stem: Which capabilities are provided by Amazon Bedrock Guardrails? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Guardrails support moderation controls such as sensitive information filtering and denied topics.

**C** is incorrect because replication is not a Guardrails feature.

**D** is incorrect because warehouse autoscaling is unrelated to AWS Bedrock.

[Amazon Bedrock Guardrails](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/guardrails.html)

questions.stem: A call center app receives steady, predictable traffic during business hours and strict p95 latency targets. Why would Provisioned Throughput be preferred over only on-demand invocations?

options.text: It reserves inference capacity for more predictable performance
It eliminates the need for IAM policies

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Provisioned Throughput is intended for reserved inference capacity and more consistent performance under predictable demand.

**B** is incorrect because it does not perform data labeling.

**C** is incorrect because IAM is still required.

**D** is incorrect because observability remains necessary.

[Provisioned Throughput for Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/prov-throughput.html)

questions.stem: Which Bedrock feature is explicitly designed for comparing model quality with automated and human evaluation methods?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because model evaluation is the Bedrock feature for quality comparison and assessment.

**B** is incorrect because prompt caching is a latency/cost feature.

**C** is incorrect because action groups expose tools.

**D** is incorrect because Provisioned Throughput reserves capacity.

[Amazon Bedrock model evaluation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-evaluation.html)

questions.stem: A team is building an enterprise assistant with Bedrock Agents. Which components are commonly part of the design? (Select TWO)

options.text: A knowledge source for retrieval when needed
EBS Multi-Attach volumes for prompt memory

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because agents commonly combine tool-calling through action groups with retrieval from approved knowledge sources.

**C** is incorrect because EBS volumes are unrelated to Bedrock agent memory patterns.

**D** is incorrect because route tables do not classify user intent.

[Amazon Bedrock Agents and Knowledge Bases](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/agents.html)

questions.stem: A developer needs to know whether a Bedrock model supports streaming responses and tool use before coding. Where should they check first?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because the model features documentation lists supported capabilities by model, including inference-related features.

**B** is incorrect because Trusted Advisor does not document model features.

**C** is incorrect because bucket policy is unrelated.

**D** is incorrect because route tables are unrelated.

[Amazon Bedrock base model features](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/models-features.html)

questions.stem: A RAG application returns an answer that is fluent but unsupported by retrieved documents. What problem is this?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because a fluent answer that is not supported by retrieved evidence indicates poor grounding and a hallucination risk.

**B** is incorrect because caching concerns reuse, not factual support.

**C** is incorrect because throughput reservations do not affect grounding by themselves.

**D** is incorrect because CloudWatch sampling is irrelevant.

[Knowledge Bases for Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/knowledge-base.html)

questions.stem: A team wants dashboards and alarms for latency and operational errors in its Bedrock-powered application. Which AWS service should they use?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because CloudWatch is the primary AWS observability service for metrics, logs, alarms, and dashboards.

**B** is incorrect because ACM manages TLS certificates.

**C** is incorrect because Cognito manages identities.

**D** is incorrect because Budgets tracks spend, not full operational telemetry.

[Monitoring AWS resources with Amazon CloudWatch](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/WhatIsCloudWatch.html)

questions.stem: Before releasing a prompt update, which checks are most valuable? (Select TWO)

options.text: Run a benchmark set that represents production tasks
Review safety outcomes on adversarial prompts
Assume unchanged behavior because the model is the same
Skip evaluation if unit tests pass

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because prompt updates should be validated on representative and adversarial cases for both utility and safety.

**C** is incorrect because small prompt changes can materially change outputs.

**D** is incorrect because unit tests alone do not measure model quality.

[Amazon Bedrock model evaluation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-evaluation.html)

questions.stem: A developer wants to stream generated content to the client as it is produced. Which invocation style should be preferred?

options.text: A streaming Bedrock API such as `ConverseStream`

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because streaming inference APIs are designed to deliver partial outputs as generation occurs.

**B** is incorrect because Glue jobs are batch ETL.

**C** is incorrect because Inventory reports list objects.

**D** is incorrect because DataSync transfers files.

[Amazon Bedrock Converse API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/conversation-inference-call.html)

questions.stem: Which feature best matches the requirement to answer questions over a curated document corpus with managed retrieval and citations?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Knowledge Bases are the managed Bedrock feature for retrieval over curated enterprise data.

**B** is incorrect because throughput concerns capacity.

**C** is incorrect because guardrails concern moderation.

**D** is incorrect because Budgets concerns spend tracking.

[Knowledge Bases for Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/knowledge-base.html)

questions.stem: Which statement about Guardrails is correct?

options.text: They help enforce safety policies across model interactions
They train a custom model from scratch

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Guardrails are the Bedrock control plane for safety policies such as content filtering and sensitive-information handling.

**B** is incorrect because training a custom model is a different capability.

**C** is incorrect because Redshift provisioning is unrelated.

**D** is incorrect because authentication is still an application and IAM concern.

[Amazon Bedrock Guardrails](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/guardrails.html)

questions.stem: Which choices improve operational efficiency for a stable high-volume GenAI app? (Select TWO)

options.text: Use Provisioned Throughput for predictable capacity
Monitor latency and error metrics in CloudWatch
Increase maximum output tokens without need
Bypass all retrieval steps regardless of task

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because reserved capacity and strong observability improve efficiency and SLA management.

**C** is incorrect because larger outputs usually add cost and latency.

**D** is incorrect because retrieval may be essential for grounding and correctness.

[Provisioned Throughput for Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/prov-throughput.html)

questions.stem: A product owner asks for human preference ratings between two prompt strategies. Which Bedrock capability addresses this need most directly?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Bedrock model evaluation supports human-based evaluation workflows for comparative assessment.

**B** is incorrect because caching affects efficiency, not ratings.

**C** is incorrect because word filters moderate content but do not gather preference judgments.

**D** is incorrect because Access Analyzer reviews permissions.

[Amazon Bedrock model evaluation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-evaluation.html)

questions.stem: A team wants an agent to book travel by consulting external systems and then summarizing the result for the user. What is the core reason to use Bedrock Agents instead of only direct model invocation?

options.text: Agents can orchestrate tool use through defined action groups
Agents always cost less than any direct invocation
Agents remove the need for IAM roles

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Agents add orchestration for multi-step tasks and tool use, which is not provided by a plain one-shot model call.

**B** is incorrect because cost depends on workload and design.

**C** is incorrect because IAM is still required.

**D** is incorrect because agents do not automatically fine-tune models.

[Amazon Bedrock Agents](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/agents.html)

questions.stem: A developer is designing a global application and discovers the preferred model is not available in one Region. Which actions are reasonable next steps? (Select TWO)

options.text: Check Bedrock model availability and features by Region
Evaluate another supported model that meets requirements
Assume all models are globally identical and continue
Move the entire application to AWS Outposts to unlock model access

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because the correct response is to verify availability and choose a supported model that still meets the workload requirements.

**C** is incorrect because availability differs by model and Region.

**D** is incorrect because Outposts is not the normal answer for Bedrock model availability.

[Amazon Bedrock base model features](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/models-features.html)

questions.stem: Which controls help reduce the risk of exposing sensitive data in prompts or outputs? (Select TWO)

options.text: Publicly shared debug logs with full prompts

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because safety filtering and least-privilege access reduce exposure risk both in model interactions and surrounding integrations.

**C** is incorrect because public debug logs increase exposure risk.

**D** is incorrect because wildcard permissions violate least privilege.

[Security in Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/security.html)

questions.stem: Which statement about prompt caching is most accurate?

options.text: It is most useful when substantial prompt context repeats across requests
It is required before using Guardrails
It stores vectors for every knowledge base automatically

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because prompt caching is valuable when repeated context would otherwise be reprocessed on many requests.

**B** is incorrect because evaluation measures quality.

**C** is incorrect because Guardrails do not depend on caching.

**D** is incorrect because knowledge-base vector storage is a separate concern.

[Prompt caching in Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/prompt-caching.html)

questions.stem: Which activity belongs most clearly in the testing and validation domain for a GenAI system?

options.text: Measuring answer quality on representative prompts
Changing the VPC DHCP option set

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because testing and validation focus on measuring the behavior and quality of model-driven outputs.

**B** is incorrect because DHCP settings are networking administration.

**C** is incorrect because storage classes are data lifecycle decisions.

**D** is incorrect because hosted zones are DNS configuration.

[Amazon Bedrock model evaluation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-evaluation.html)

questions.stem: Which capabilities are commonly associated with Amazon Bedrock Agents? (Select TWO)

options.text: Reasoning over user intent to decide tool use
Provisioning an Amazon Redshift cluster automatically

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Bedrock Agents help interpret requests and invoke configured tools through action groups.

**C** is incorrect because cluster provisioning is unrelated.

**D** is incorrect because agents are not a universal replacement for all orchestration services.

[Amazon Bedrock Agents](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/agents.html)
